summary: All three round 1 blocking issues are correctly resolved. The two non-blocking minors (R1-4, R1-5) are also fixed. Implementation is production-ready.

round1_disposition.verification: core.ts:526 now binds `const { recoveredFrom } = verifyResult` after verify succeeds. settleOnChain receives `recoveredFrom` as second arg (line 530). X-PAYMENT-RESPONSE payer field is `recoveredFrom` (line 557). New test at core.test.ts:336 exercises a wire `authorization.from = 0x9999...` vs verify-returned `recoveredFrom = 0xaaaa...` and asserts both settle's second positional arg and the decoded payer field are `0xaaaa...`. Semantically correct: verify-failure path still uses `payload.payload.authorization.from` for the payerHash (correct — no authenticated address exists when signature is invalid).

core.ts:47 imports `parseUsdPrice` from `./x402.js`. Line 415 calls `parseUsdPrice(opts.price)`. Private `parsePriceToBaseUnits`, `USDC_DECIMALS`, `USDC_SCALE`, `PRICE_RE` are deleted. New test at core.test.ts:557 calls `paywall` with `price: '-1'` and asserts `rejects.toBeInstanceOf(InvalidPriceError)`. Confirms `InvalidPriceError` propagates unchanged from `x402.ts`.

index.ts no longer imports from `./core.js` at all. `SecurityLogger`, `SecurityEventCatalog`, `SecurityEventName` are moved to `types.ts` and re-exported from there. `PaywallConfig` in `types.ts:49` now includes `logger?: SecurityLogger`. `PaywallCoreOptions` in `core.ts` is now an alias for `PaywallConfig` (still internal to core). Public value surface confirmed as `['NETWORKS','OpaqueRelayerKey','fastifyPaywall','withPaywall']`. Public type surface is `ExactEvmPayload`, `NetworkConfig`, `OpaqueRelayerKeyShape`, `PaymentPayload`, `PaymentRequirements`, `PaywallConfig`, `SecurityEventCatalog`, `SecurityEventName`, `SecurityLogger` — all from `types.ts` or `relayer-key.ts`. Clean.

fastify.test.ts:84 now uses a `routeHandlerCalls = vi.fn()` spy. Test at line 93 asserts `routeHandlerCalls` was called exactly once + status 200 + correct body + correct header. This genuinely proves the preHandler did not short-circuit via reply.send on the passthrough path.

core.test.ts:319 adds 'factory-state RPC error with non-stale cache returns last-good value (passthrough)'. Test warms cache on first request, then mocks all future readContract calls to reject, issues second request within 5s TTL, and asserts `second.kind === 'passthrough'`. Covers the missing non-stale-fallback branch.
Content-Type header casing inconsistency between core.ts and errors.ts remains. Accepted as informational — not a correctness issue.

notes: The verify-failure path correctly uses payload.payload.authorization.from (not recoveredFrom) for payerHash in the switch block — this is semantically correct since no authenticated address exists when signature verification fails.
logger field moved to PaywallConfig in types.ts cleanly integrates with the existing type hierarchy and keeps SecurityLogger on the public surface without pulling in any core-internal types.
196/196 tests passing per orchestrator report. Typecheck and lint clean. Build smoke confirms expected public surface.
